post.description:
<!-- theme: warning -->
> 📘 **Scopes required**:
> - ical:write

Create a new external iCal import for a property. This allows you to import bookings into a Hospitable property from an external iCal calendar feed. Useful for OTAs that we do not have a native integration with.

Adding a new iCal feed will trigger an import of all bookings from the feed within 1 minute.

url.description: The URL of the iCal feed to import.

name.description: A friendly name for this iCal import.

host.description: Host information for the iCal import.

items.example: You cannot use a local/testing domain for iCal imports.

You have already added this iCal feed on another property.

parameters.description: The UUID of the property.

parameters.name: icalUuid
parameters.description: The UUID of the iCal import.

put.description:
<!-- theme: warning -->
> 📘 **Scopes required**:
> - ical:write

> **Access Control**:
> Only PAT tokens and the same vendor that created the iCal import can edit it.

Update an existing external iCal import for a property. You can update the URL, name, and host information. Editing the URL will trigger a re-import of the iCal feed. If future bookings on the previous URL are not present in the new URL, they will be marked as cancelled in our system.

url.description: The URL of the iCal feed to import.

name.description: An optional friendly name for this iCal import. Can be null. This is shown in the Hospitable UI when viewing the property.

host.description: Host information for the iCal import.

first_name.description: An optional first name for the host. Can be null. If null, Hospitable will use host name data from other connected accounts on that property. This is used for short codes in messaging rules.

last_name.description: An optional last name for the host. Can be null. If null, Hospitable will use host name data from other connected accounts on that property. This is used for short codes in messaging rules.

resync.description: Optional field that can be excluded. Useful if you have fixed the underlying URL and the URL hasn't changed, and you want Hospitable to reconnect and re-fetch the iCal feed. This operation is throttled to a maximum of once every 15 minutes per feed.

items.example: You cannot use a local/testing domain for iCal imports.

items.example: We could not find a valid iCal feed at that link.

taxes.description: All the taxes applicable to the quote.

fees.description: All the fees applicable to the quote.

discounts.description: Any discounts applicable to the quote.

sub_total.description: The total price of the quote. This is the accommodation + fees + taxes - discounts.

total_fees.description: The combined total price of the fees for the quote.

total.description: The total price of the quote. This is the accommodation + fees + taxes - discounts.

total_without_taxes.description: The total price of the quote without taxes. This is the accommodation + fees - discounts.

put.description:
<!-- theme: warning -->
> 🚧 **Requires Vendor access**
> 
> This endpoint is restricted to Vendors only. To become a vendor, [learn more here](authentication-3#applying-to-become-an-approved-vendor)

> 📘 **Required scopes:**
> 
> - enrichment:write
> - enrichment:shortcode:[key]
>
>
> **Available shortcodes:**
> - `guidebook`
> - `rental_agreement`
> - `smartlock_code`
> - `upsell`
> Please reach out to team-platform@hospitable.com to request for a shortcode.

Vendors are responsible for communicating to their users that they must put a slight delay on their Hospitable messaging rule if it is to be sent when a booking is placed. This is to prevent a message from being sent out just before a shortcode enrichment is processed (which happens within seconds from the PUT request).

value.description: The new value of the shortcode. Must be either a string or null, max 255 chars.

description.example: A link to your property guidebook, which will be populated anytime the %guidebook% short code is used in your messaging rules.

post.description:
<!-- theme: warning -->
> 📘 Vendors must request for this scope to be added:
> 
> - reservation:write

Create a new manual reservation. This endpoint allows the creation of manual reservations with complete control over guest information, dates, and financials.

Date validations:
- minimum stay rules are ignored for Personal Access Tokens
- availability status is ignored for Personal Access Tokens (i.e. a manual reservation can still be created over blocked dates)
- a manual reservation cannot be created over existing bookings

> 📘 **Available includes in response:**
> 
> - guest
> - user
> - financials (required: `financials:read` scope)
> - listings (required: `listing:read` scope)
> - properties (required: `property:read` scope)
> - review (required: `reviews:read` scope)

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated values, any of: guest,user,financials,listings,properties,review

property_id.description: The UUID of the property for this reservation

check_in.description: Check-in date in YYYY-MM-DD format. The date must be available for booking.

check_out.description: Check-out date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Must be after check_in date and all dates in the range must be available for booking.

reservation_code.description:
Optional field. A code will be generated in Hospitable when this is not provided.

This is a unique identifier that is visible in the Hospitable UI. This is different from the reservation uuid that is returned in the API and used in your queries.

language.description: Guest's preferred language code (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'fr'). Must be one of the supported language codes.

reason_phrase.example: Some dates are unavailable for booking

unavailable_dates.description: List of dates that are unavailable for booking

errors.description: A map of field names to validation error messages

put.description:
Update an existing manual reservation. Only manual reservations can be updated.

<!-- theme: warning -->
> 📘 **Required scopes:**
> 
> - reservation:write


<!-- theme: warning -->
>  **Important Restrictions:**
> 
> * **Date Availability:** If the new dates are not available for booking, the endpoint will return a 400 error with metadata containing the specific unavailable dates.
>   Note the following validation exemptions:
>     - minimum stay rules are ignored for Personal Access Tokens
>     - availability status is ignored for Personal Access Tokens (i.e. a manual reservation can still be created over blocked dates)
> * **Guest Information:** Guest data cannot be updated if manual changes have been made to the guest information. Attempting to do so will return a 422 validation error.

>  **Access Control**:
> Only PAT tokens or the vendor that created the reservation can update it.

> 📘 **Available includes in response:**
> 
> - guest
> - user
> - financials (required: `financials:read` scope)
> - listings (required: `listing:read` scope)
> - properties (required: `property:read` scope)
> - review (required: `reviews:read` scope)

parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: The UUID of the reservation to update

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated values, any of: guest,user,financials,listings,properties,review

check_in.description: Check-in date in YYYY-MM-DD format. The date must be available for booking.

check_out.description: Check-out date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Must be after check_in date and all dates in the range must be available for booking.

reason_phrase.example: Some dates are unavailable for booking

unavailable_dates.description: List of dates that are unavailable for booking

403.description: Forbidden - You do not have permission to update this reservation

reason_phrase.example: You do not have permission to update this reservation.

errors.description: A map of field names to validation error messages

guest.email: The guest.email field must be a valid email address.
guest.first_name: Cannot update guest information as changes have been made manually.

post.description:
> **Rate Limits**: 
> - Only 2 messages per minute can be sent to a single reservation
> - Only 50 messages can be sent every 5 minutes

parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: The UUID of the reservation

body.description:
Text body of the message to send.
> - HTML is not supported.
> - `/n` is parsed for line breaks.

images.description:
The URL location of any images to attach to the message.

> - Maximum of 3 images.
> - The maximum filesize is 5MB.

sender_id.description:
This is the co-host user id. When provided, the message will be sent on behalf of this co-host rather than the default host (listing owner). This can be left blank, in which case will send the message as the listing owner.

> - Only supported for Airbnb reservations
> - To find this, use the property endpoint with the listing include. It is found in `listings.co_hosts.user_id`.

sent_reference_id.description: A unique identfier that can be used to match a send message request with a `Message` resource.

sender_id: The sender_id is not valid for this Reservation
